content: We can repurpose ICM's intrinsic curiosity reward signal from single autonomous agent exploration as a dynamic reputation scoring mechanism for interoperable agents communicating via the Agent Communication Protocol (ACP), where agents gain higher 'curiosity-based reputation' for sharing novel, under-explored information that other agents have not yet discovered in the open interoperable network. This creates a self-organizing incentive for ACP-connected agent swarms to collectively explore new problem spaces without centralized reward design.
